# devcompass

> Local-first GitHub command center for developers working across multiple orgs and repos.

devcompass is a browser-based dashboard that aggregates all your GitHub activity — repos, PRs, commits, issues, and CI checks — across every organization you belong to, in one fast UI. There is no backend server. Your GitHub Personal Access Token never leaves your browser.

## What it does

- **Digest view**: Activity heatmap + stat tiles (24h / 7d / 30d) across all your repos
- **Needs-me inbox**: Every PR waiting on your review or approval, with snooze support
- **Since-last-visit feed**: Diff of what changed in your repos while you were away
- **All repos view**: Full list of repos across all orgs, with pinning and filtering
- **Repo detail**: Commits with branch chips, open PRs, issues, releases, and CI check status
- **PR detail**: Full PR view with markdown rendering and review actions
- **Local cache**: IndexedDB stores everything — reloads paint instantly without re-fetching

## How to use it

1. Open the hosted demo at https://devcompass.vercel.app or self-host (see below)
2. Generate a GitHub Classic PAT with `repo` and `read:org` scopes
3. Paste the token in the app — it is stored in `localStorage` and sent only to `api.github.com`
4. The digest loads immediately; repos and orgs sync in the background

If orgs appear missing, the token likely needs explicit SSO authorization for those orgs. The Config → Token tab shows the SSO header to help diagnose this.

## Requirements

- A GitHub Classic Personal Access Token (PAT) with scopes: `repo`, `read:org`
- A modern browser (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge)
- No account, no signup, no server

## Self-hosting

```bash
git clone https://github.com/dPeluChe/devcompass
cd devcompass
npm install
npm run dev        # dev server at http://localhost:8099
npm run build      # production bundle
```

v0.5.0 · MIT licensed. Static bundle — no server, no database, no DevOps.

## Privacy

- Token stored in `localStorage` — sent only to `api.github.com`
- Repo and PR data cached in IndexedDB on your machine (TTL-bound, auto-pruned)
- No analytics, no telemetry, no third-party tracking scripts
- No external storage of any kind
